title: VerdictRecompute
description: Server-side verdict recomputation contract (PRD §9). Given the checkpoint results a Pad submitted plus the standard revision and bundle version they were computed against, the server independently recomputes the verdict and reports any disagreement with the Pad-submitted verdict. Used to detect false-pass / drift and to drive requalification. This file defines both the request and response objects.

verdict.description: The three terminal verdicts (fail-closed; review_required is never auto-promoted to pass).

checkpointResultStatus.description: Per-checkpoint outcome status. Anything other than 'pass' contributes to review_required unless it is 'fail'.

inspection_id.description: Optional id of the Pad inspection being audited.

standard_revision_id.description: Revision the Pad computed against; the server recomputes against this same revision.

bundle_version.description: Bundle version installed on the Pad (from the signed manifest).

submitted_verdict.description: The overall verdict the Pad submitted; the diff is measured against this.

checkpoint_results.description: The Pad-submitted per-checkpoint results the server recomputes from.

recomputed_verdict.description: Verdict the server independently recomputed from the submitted checkpoint results and the standard revision.

diff.description: Structured disagreement between the Pad-submitted and server-recomputed evaluation.

verdict_changed.description: True iff the overall verdict differs.

false_pass_suspected.description: True when the Pad submitted 'pass' but the server recomputed 'fail' or 'review_required' (PRD §9 false-pass trigger).

checkpoint_diffs.description: Per-checkpoint disagreements only (points that agree are omitted).

unknown_point_codes.description: Submitted point_codes not present in the standard revision (fail-closed: forces review_required).

missing_point_codes.description: Point_codes required by the revision but absent from the submission (fail-closed: forces review_required).
